summary:
The following are the key takeaways from Lockheed Martin's earnings conference call:
- Record backlog of $179 billion, the highest in company history, with book-to-bill ratio of 1.7x in Q3
- Raised full-year 2025 guidance: sales midpoint increased $250 million to $74.5 billion (5% organic growth), segment operating profit to $6.675-$6.725 billion, and EPS to $22.15-$22.35
- PAC-3 multiyear contract of $9.8 billion for nearly 2,000 MSE interceptors - the largest PAC-3 MSE contract in history
- JASSM/LRASM large lot procurement contract definitized at $9.5 billion with U.S. Air Force and Navy
- CH-53K King Stallion received $10.9 billion multiyear contract for up to 99 helicopters - largest award in RMS history
- F-35 Lot 18/19 definitized adding $11 billion and 151 aircraft; now expecting 175-190 deliveries in 2025 (essentially 1 per working day)
- Free cash flow of $3.3 billion in Q3, bringing YTD total to $4.1 billion; $6.6 billion full-year target maintained
- Board approved 5% dividend increase (23rd consecutive year) and increased share repurchase authorization
- Space backlog reached new high of $38 billion; MFC sales up 14% YoY with PAC-3 eclipsing $2 billion YTD (up 18%)
- Well-positioned for Golden Dome homeland defense initiative with existing products in space-based interceptors, radars, missiles, and C2 systems; planning on-orbit SBI demonstration by 2028
